steps.title: Identify what the text shows
steps.content: Social media users invented the "meng-" prefix online, then that prefix spread into spoken Bahasa Indonesia. The passage also notes similar examples in other languages.

steps.title: Find the logical conclusion
steps.content: Because language changes were created on social media and then moved into everyday speech, social media is doing more than just observing change — it is helping cause it.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘is more useful for studying informal language than for studying formal or official language.’: the text never compares formal vs. informal study; this is unsupported. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘appears to be exerting an exceptionally strong influence on the evolution of Bahasa Indonesia.’: the final sentence says similar things happen in many languages, so Bahasa Indonesia is not exceptional. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘may give linguists a somewhat misleading sense of how languages are changing.’: nothing in the text suggests linguists are being misled; the example is presented as a real change. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘does not merely register changes in language usage but can facilitate such changes.’: matches exactly — social media both records and helps cause the change. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
